summary: Phase 4 release: protected project recovery journal, lifecycle-safe local saves, WebP-to-PNG export fallback, Android-safe backup delivery, save-before-update enforcement and touch-first onboarding polish.
features: A bounded same-tab recovery journal protects the newest drawing state during refresh, renderer restart, background-tab eviction or a save interruption; it is restored only when newer than the persisted project.
Autosave now captures a protected draft immediately, flushes on page lifecycle transitions and reports a timestamped successful local save after IndexedDB commits.
Export saves the current project before output and falls back from unsupported WebP encoding to a valid PNG rather than failing the creator workflow.
Backup downloads retain their object URL long enough for delayed Android browser downloads, while update application checks that the project save completed before navigation.
Onboarding restores focus and accepts keyboard or horizontal touch navigation without changing the premium RTL/mobile visual system.

buildPolicy.fallback: npm run model:sync remains recovery-only; production clients use the verified local asset.
buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: no remote MediaPipe module or model is loaded by the deployed browser; Phase 3 tracking and continuity run locally.
buildPolicy.projectSafety: Recovery journaling is session-scoped and bounded. It never uploads drawing data and is cleared after a verified local persistence commit.
buildPolicy.export: Exports save the current project first. Unsupported WebP encoding falls back to a PNG file locally.
buildPolicy.updates: Applying a release requires a completed local save before service-worker promotion or cache-busted navigation.
